audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for meekness/gentleness of Messiah, spiritual warfare imagery, taking thoughts captive, limits of authority, boasting in Lord.

verses.restored: Now I myself, Paul, appeal to you through the meekness and gentleness of Messiah, I who am humble among you face to face, but absent am bold toward you.
I ask that when present I may not have to be bold with the confidence with which I calculate to dare against some who calculate us as walking according to flesh.
For though walking in flesh, we do not wage war according to flesh.
For the weapons of our warfare are not fleshly, but powerful by the Divine for tearing down strongholds,
tearing down arguments and every height lifted up against the knowledge of the Divine, and taking every thought captive into the obedience of Messiah,
and being ready to correct every disobedience, when your obedience is fulfilled.
Look at what is before your face. If anyone is persuaded in oneself to be of Messiah, let one calculate this again for oneself: that just as one is of Messiah, so also are we.
For even if I boast somewhat more about our authority, which the Lord gave for building up and not for tearing you down, I will not be ashamed,
so that I may not seem as if I would terrify you through letters.
For they say, His letters are weighty and strong, but his bodily presence is weak and his speech has been despised.
Let such a one calculate this: what we are in word through letters when absent, such also we are in work when present.

For we do not dare to classify or compare ourselves with some of those recommending themselves; but they, measuring themselves by themselves and comparing themselves with themselves, do not understand.
But we will not boast beyond measure, but according to the measure of the boundary that the Divine apportioned to us, a measure to reach even as far as you.
For we are not overextending ourselves as though not reaching to you; for we came even as far as you in the good news of Messiah.
Not boasting beyond measure in others' labors, but having hope that as your trust grows, we will be enlarged among you according to our boundary into abundance,
to announce good news into the regions beyond you, not to boast in another's boundary in things already prepared.
But the one boasting, let that one boast in the Lord.
For not the one recommending oneself is approved, but the one whom the Lord recommends.

translation_notes: Spiritual warfare imagery is aimed at arguments, pride, and thought-patterns opposed to Divine knowledge, not at harming people.
Authority is explicitly for building up, not tearing down.
